title: Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi Lays Foundation Stone, Inaugurates Development Projects Worth Over Rs. 53,400 Crore in Bhuj, Gujarat
content:
Prime Minister Narendra Modi, during his visit to Bhuj on 26th May 2025, spotlighted Kutchâ€™s pivotal role in Indiaâ€™s green energy revolution. He laid the foundation stone for a new green hydrogen plant in the region, proudly noting that the technology used is fully â€œMade in India.â€ Kandla has been designated one of the three national hubs for green hydrogen, reflecting Indiaâ€™s ambition to lead in next-generation clean fuels. Modi underlined the transformational role of green hydrogen, describing it as the â€œfuel of the futureâ€ that will soon power vehicles, homes, and cities. In addition to hydrogen, Kutch is home to the massive Khavda Renewable Energy Park, one of the worldâ€™s largest solar energy projects, placing the region firmly on the global clean energy map. These developments, he noted, not only align with Indiaâ€™s green goals but also promise to bring major employment and investment opportunities to the region. 
The Prime Minister also reiterated the government's broader commitment to ensuring clean and affordable electricity for all through initiatives like the PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana. With its vast solar and wind potential, Kutch stands at the heart of India's mission to become a global leader in renewable energy and the green economy.
author: Analysts Account
